title: First Insurance Act
desc: India’s first insurance legislation enacted to regulate the growing insurance market.

title: Indian Insurance Companies Act
desc: Introduced government supervision over insurance company operations and investments.

title: Comprehensive Insurance Act
desc: Consolidated earlier laws into a single framework governing life and non-life insurance.

title: Life Insurance Nationalized
desc: 175 private life insurers merged into the Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC).

title: General Insurance Nationalized
desc: Non-life sector consolidated under the General Insurance Corporation of India.

title: Economic Liberalization
desc: India’s economic reforms opened the door for insurance sector restructuring.

title: IRDA Established
desc: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority formed as the sector regulator.

title: Private Insurers Enter
desc: Private companies allowed to operate, ending the LIC monopoly after four decades.

title: IRDAI Reconstituted
desc: Renamed to IRDAI with expanded powers to drive inclusive insurance growth.

title: FDI Limit Raised to 74%
desc: Foreign direct investment cap increased to attract global insurers to India.

title: IRDAI Regulatory Reforms
desc: Bima Vistaar, composite licensing, and risk-based capital framework approved.

title: 100% FDI in Insurance
desc: Full foreign ownership permitted, opening India’s insurance market globally.

title: Insurance for All by 2047
desc: India’s roadmap to achieve universal insurance coverage by independence centenary.
